arguments.task: Open the official Harvest API documentation and related official Harvest pages needed to verify these fields for the Harvest app: one-line description of the product/API, all documented API authentication methods, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing or plan requirements for API access, and whether an official MCP server exists. Prefer first-party documentation and pricing/help pages. Read page content directly; do not rely on search snippets. Return the canonical URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. If needed, navigate from the API docs to auth, rate limit, and pricing pages on getharvest.com or help.getharvest.com.

input.thought: Open official Harvest docs and extract evidence from first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Harvest is time tracking and invoicing software with an API for managing time, projects, tasks, users, invoices, expenses, estimates, and reports.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official API docs document two auth options for API V2: OAuth 2.0 applications and personal access tokens. Requests use a Bearer access token and also require the Harvest-Account-Id header.
raw_result.gating_reason: Harvest publishes self-serve API V2 documentation and a public pricing page; the gathered evidence did not show an approval or partner gate for initial API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public API V2 covers core Harvest resources including users, clients, contacts, assignments, tasks, time entries, expenses, invoices, estimates, roles, projects, and reports.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Harvest MCP server was verified in the gathered first-party evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs indicate general API requests are limited to 100 requests per 15 seconds and Reports API requests to 100 requests per 15 minutes; responses may include Retry-After information when throttled.
raw_result.notable_signals: Harvest provides current API V2 docs with separate authentication guidance, enumerated resource areas, and published rate-limiting guidance, which are strong signals for near-term toolkit buildability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists was not supported by direct first-party evidence from a canonical Harvest page in the gathered set; absence was inferred from not finding an official MCP page, so that field should be human-checked.

result.description: Harvest is time tracking and invoicing software with an API for managing time, projects, tasks, users, invoices, expenses, estimates, and reports.
result.auth_notes: The official API docs document two auth options for API V2: OAuth 2.0 applications and personal access tokens. Requests use a Bearer access token and also require the Harvest-Account-Id header.
result.gating_reason: Harvest publishes self-serve API V2 documentation and a public pricing page; the gathered evidence did not show an approval or partner gate for initial API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public API V2 covers core Harvest resources including users, clients, contacts, assignments, tasks, time entries, expenses, invoices, estimates, roles, projects, and reports.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Harvest MCP server was verified in the gathered first-party evidence.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs indicate general API requests are limited to 100 requests per 15 seconds and Reports API requests to 100 requests per 15 minutes; responses may include Retry-After information when throttled.
result.notable_signals: Harvest provides current API V2 docs with separate authentication guidance, enumerated resource areas, and published rate-limiting guidance, which are strong signals for near-term toolkit buildability.
